evals.prompt: Do a deep research dive on whether intermittent fasting improves insulin sensitivity in non-diabetic adults.
evals.expected_output: PI frames a sharp research question, builds a paper corpus via search_papers.py across multiple sources, spawns hypothesis-generator sub-agents, tests each hypothesis with a Proponent/Falsifier pair, synthesizes, and writes RESEARCH.md with real citations.
expectations: Restates the topic as a specific, answerable research question
Runs scripts/search_papers.py with one or more query strings to build corpus.json
Creates a durable workspace folder research/{slug}/ with a README status board, 00-question.md, and data/ before spawning sub-agents
Parallel sub-agents each write their own file (e.g. 04-evidence/H1-falsifier.md); the PI is the sole writer of the README, 02-hypotheses.md, and 05-decision-log.md (no shared-file clobbering)
Spawns 3-5 hypothesis-generator sub-agents in parallel, each with a distinct stance
Fetches full text (scripts/fetch_fulltext.py) for the papers the surviving hypotheses cite, producing a manifest.json that marks each paper full_text / abstract_only / failed
Each surviving hypothesis is tested by a matched Proponent and Falsifier sub-agent (adversarial, not confirmation-only) that read full text where available
Assigns a Supported/Refuted/Inconclusive verdict per hypothesis calibrated to evidence quality and read depth
Verdicts are recorded in 05-decision-log.md as PROPOSED, then RATIFIED only after a red-team pass challenges the synthesis (06-red-team.md)
RESEARCH.md is written at the workspace root with a TL;DR, novel insight, hypotheses table, and a References list of real corpus papers (DOIs/URLs) tagged [full text] or [abstract only]
No fabricated citations — every cited paper appears in the corpus
Ends with a DONE: research/{slug}/RESEARCH.md completion line

evals.prompt: What does the science say about microplastics crossing the blood-brain barrier? Research this topic for me.
evals.expected_output: Triggers on 'what does the science say' + 'research this topic'. Prioritizes biomedical sources, runs the hypothesis -> test -> synthesis pipeline, reports honest confidence given likely thin/emerging evidence.
expectations: Triggers the researcher skill
Builds corpus emphasizing biomedical sources (europepmc/openalex) given the topic
Falsifier phase genuinely attempts to refute, mining full-text Methods/Results for confounds and methodological weaknesses where open-access full text is available
Paywalled papers with no open-access copy are marked abstract_only and weighted lower rather than dropped or overstated
Confidence is calibrated; an 'Inconclusive' verdict is treated as a valid result, not a failure
RESEARCH.md includes a 'Novel Insight' section and a best open question

evals.expected_output: Does NOT trigger researcher. A quick factual lookup with a known answer — no literature search, hypotheses, or sub-agents.
expectations: Does NOT invoke the researcher skill
Answers directly without spawning sub-agents or building a corpus

evals.prompt: Summarize this paper I'm pasting below: [abstract text...]
evals.expected_output: Does NOT trigger researcher. The user supplied a single source to summarize; there is no literature search or hypothesis-testing pipeline to run.
expectations: Does NOT invoke the researcher skill for a single user-supplied paper
Summarizes the provided text directly
